title: arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. subcordata
intro: Arctostaphylos crustacea subsp. subcordata, also known as Bigberry Manzanita, is a shrub species in the heath family, Ericaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, offering various health benefits, including boosting the immune system, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Bigberry Manzanita include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support overall well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
